channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Trump's AI-Generated Signs Attempt to Shift Focus from Taylor Swift Wedding
item.description: President Donald Trump faced widespread ridicule after his administration posted AI-generated signs at Madison Square Garden, attempting to divert attention from Taylor Swift and NFL player Travis Kel...

item.title: Legal Debate Over Public Access to BIR Tax Records in Duterte's Impeachment Case
item.description: The House prosecutor and Manila 3rd District Representative Joel Chua argued that the sealed BIR tax records of Vice President Sara Duterte and her husband Manases Carpio should be opened publicly dur...

item.title: Meghalaya Chief Minister Highlights Severe Rainfall Deficit and Urges Preparedness for El Nino and Climate Change Impacts
item.description: Meghalaya Chief Minister Conrad K Sangma has raised serious concerns about the impact of climate change and the possible arrival of El Nino phenomenon on the state's weather patterns. Speaking at a wo...

item.title: Gachagua Denies Endorsement of Musyoka as Opposition Candidate, Warns Against Ethnic Coalitions
item.description: DCP leader Rigathi Gachagua has refuted claims that Kalonzo Musyoka has been chosen as the United Opposition's presidential candidate ahead of the 2027 elections. Speaking from his Wamunyoro residence...

item.title: Nigeria's IPI Urges Release of Journalist Stanley Ugagbe After Unlawful Arrest
item.description: The Nigerian National Committee of the International Press Institute (IPI Nigeria) has demanded the immediate release of journalist Stanley Ugagbe, who was arrested by security operatives after his ne...

item.title: Iran and Japan Discuss Oil Sales Amid Sanctions Waiver and Safety Concerns
item.description: Iran is engaging in discussions with Japanese companies to resume oil sales under a U.S. sanctions waiver, though buyers are seeking an extended waiver and assurances about shipping safety. The curren...

item.title: South Africa and England Begin Nations Championship Campaign with Rivalry at Ellis Park
item.description: The article details the upcoming rugby match between South Africa and England as part of the Nations Championship, highlighting the challenges both teams face. South Africa, the world champions, are s...

item.title: Challenges Facing America's Democracy Amid Political and Social Crises
item.description: The article critiques the state of American democracy in 2026, highlighting systemic failures under President Trump. It discusses the Supreme Court's role in enabling presidential accountability evasi...

item.title: Zanzibar's Tax Revenue Surges 41% to 1.2 Trillion Shillings
item.description: The Zanzibar Revenue Authority (ZRA) reported a 41% surge in tax collections for the 2025/26 financial year, reaching 1.215 trillion shillings, which is 96.7% of its target of 1.257 trillion. This mar...

item.title: Foreign Women Describe Multi-Day Kidnapping and Assault During Pakistan Visit
item.description: Two foreign women, a Dutch citizen and her Venezuelan friend, detailed a harrowing multi-day experience involving alleged kidnapping, extortion, physical assault, and sexual abuse during their visit t...

item.title: Australia's Property Market Shifts as First Home Buyers Halt Purchases
item.description: Australia's property market is undergoing a significant transformation, with first home buyers and investors showing reduced activity. Data reveals a decline in first home loan applications, with a 13...

item.title: Ireland's Controversial Try in Rugby Match Sparks Debate
item.description: In a closely contested rugby union match, Ireland's Jamison Gibson-Park scored a try that sparked significant debate among fans and officials. The try, which occurred on the stroke of half-time, was a...

item.title: Norovirus Outbreak Affects Over 120 on Cruise Ship En Route to Canada
item.description: A norovirus outbreak on the Princess Cruises-operated Ruby Princess cruise ship affected over 120 passengers and crew members, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC). The ou...

item.title: Tanzania strengthens climate governance with 1.9bn/- international partnership
item.description: Tanzania has secured a 1.87 billion Tanzanian shilling (7 million Norwegian Kroner) partnership with the Royal Norwegian Embassy and UNDP to enhance climate governance. The initiative focuses on build...

item.title: Young Man Loses Job After TikTok Content Sparks Controversy
item.description: Daniel Seguton Chelimo, a 27-year-old from Baringo North, lost his ranching job after his employer discovered a viral TikTok video he shared. The video warned women about a scam where men tricked them...

item.title: FIFA Reschedules England-Mexico World Cup Match to 1am, Impacting Pubs and Schools
item.description: FIFA has confirmed that England's World Cup match against Mexico will proceed as originally planned at 01:00 BST on Monday, despite initial reports of a potential six-hour time shift. The decision fol...

item.title: Pope Leo Advocates for Migrant Protection in Lampedusa Amid Mediterranean Crisis
item.description: Pope Leo XIV emphasized the dignity of migrants during his visit to Lampedusa, an Italian island serving as a critical gateway to Europe. The Mediterranean, while symbolizing hope for many seeking bet...

item.title: Impeachment Trial of Vice President Sara Duterte Begins on July 6
item.description: The impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte, the first Philippine public official to face impeachment twice by the House of Representatives, is set to begin on July 6. Four articles of impeac...

item.title: New Zealand Secures Narrow Victory Over France in Rugby Clash
item.description: In a thrilling rugby encounter, New Zealand's Dave Rennie-led team secured a dramatic 34-32 win against an understrength France squad in the first Nations Championship Test. The match, played at Chris...

item.title: Bena Sareen on Book Design Evolution and AI's Role
item.description: The article features Bena Sareen, a senior book designer in India, discussing her 26-year career in publishing. She emphasizes the importance of simplicity and minimalism in book cover design, especia...
